entry.title: Re: Is it possible to switch my CDS view (odata v2) for POs to OData v4?
Re: SAP Cloud ALM support for monitoring Content server
entry.content:
@MarcinOzdzinski , thank you for your response

 

FYI, I found the following interesting notes:

1722076 - The Future of SAP MaxDB 

1178367 - SAP MaxDB: End of Support Dates 

719971 - SAP Content Server release strategy 

Note 719971 his the following interesting bits:

* SAP Content Server 7.5 (and higher) can be used with any application release, including Business Suite 7, ECC, S/4HANA, etc.

* SAP Content Server shares the same maintenance dates and strategy as SAP Business Suite 7 and SAP S/4HANA

* After the End of Maintenance of SAP MaxDB (see SAP Note 1178367 - SAP MaxDB: End of Support Dates), SAP Content Server may only be used to store documents in the file system. There are no plans to support other database systems for storage in the future

Thank you for highlighting this!

entry.title: BTE_01: Account xxxxxxxx can't be posted with affiliation 'A' (trading partner XXXXXX)
entry.content:
Hi Guru's,

User trying to create document with PO in MIRO and getting error as BTE_01: Account xxxxxxxx can't be posted with affiliation 'A' (trading partner XXXXXX).

Please advise on this

Thank you

Best Regards,

Praveen R

Can any one guide us to create a connection from Success factor workforce analytics to SAP Analytics Cloud.
we would required to know the configurations in Success factor to enable workforce analytics.

Expecting a quick support.

Thanks in Advance!

entry.title: SAP Enable Now for Fieldglass
entry.content:
Dear,
One of our customers wants to implement SAP Enable Now in their SAP Fieldgladd tenant. I believe there is a kind of package available. Can you please inform me if this is still the case? Every additional information would be welcome.

entry.title: No commitment item entered in item 00001 9000 130800
entry.content:
Hi All,

i have configured Funds management module in PSM. posted the PR,PO and GRN entry. In all the three process budget check is happening. while posting MIRO entry i am having the following error.But i am able to post vendor invoice thru-FB60.no commitment error.

This issue is commitment item is not deriving for vendor Recon. GL- 130800. But i have assigned the commitment item-5000( with FT-60 and item category-30) in the GL master, also in FMDERIVER - cost element to commitment item and Business transaction - RMWR to commitment item. Still i am getting the error as follows.

No commitment item entered in item 00001 9000 130800

Message no. FI311

Diagnosis

The posted document contains at least one company code in which Funds Management, Cash Budget Management, or Project Cash Management is active. Therefore you must enter a commitment item in all involved company codes.

 

Procedure

Enter a commitment item.

If you cannot enter a commitment item, check with your system administrator that your account assignment derivation is adjusted so that the system can automatically derive a commitment item from another account assignment, or from the G/L or cost element.

Request our members please guide me where i am going wrong.

Thanks

NKM

entry.title: Re: SD downpayment amount should include tax value

entry.title: Re: Transporting Fiori Apps in "My Home" space
entry.content: were you able to use the program advised in that note? does it only apply to "Spaces & Pages" or also to the classic Home page group? We tried based on the latter, and it hasn't worked, wondering if we're missing anything?

entry.title: Re: Opa5 FileUploader
entry.content:
Hi,

You can use the following method: fireChange.

In your case, it would look like:

 

 

return this.waitFor({
    id: "fileUploader", 
    controlType: "sap.ui.unified.FileUploader", 
    viewName: sViewName, 
    action: function (oFileUploader) {
        var data = <myData>;
        var blob = new Blob([data], { type: "text/plain" });
        var file = new Blob([blob], "data.txt", { type: "text/plain" });
        fileUploader.fireChange({ newValue: "data.txt", files: [file] });
    }
});

entry.title: What's the best way to monitor the progress of an iFlow using a PowerShell script?
entry.content:
Hi All, 

I am attempting to trigger an iFlow using an HTTP endpoint from a PowerShell script. Since it is a long-running flow, I would like to incorporate a periodic monitoring feature within the script itself.

Is there a monitoring API available that can facilitate this?

Thanks,

Himanshu

entry.title: Re: How to generate seperate spool for each customer in F.27 - Periodic account statements
entry.content:
I have created a spool that customer receives a statement of account every week using FBL5N

However, the Open at Key Date stays as the original and will not use current date when emailing to customer.  How can I get the date to change to a live date rather than using the old date and old data 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: is there any APIs are available for HANA to Park the invoice?
entry.content: What application are you using? HANA itself is the database underneath the application. A task like "parking an invoice" would be performed through the application, not directly at the database level.

entry.title: Re: Unable to determine source storage bin during repleni...
entry.content: Also make sure there is inventory to pick up from in RPPK and/or RHRK

entry.title: Re: We did it... SAP confirmed it is NIST CSF Tier 3
entry.content:
This is such an important achievement! Implementing the NIST Cybersecurity Framework (CSF) is crucial for SAP as it provides a structured approach to managing cybersecurity risks, enhancing security posture, and ensuring compliance with regulatory standards. By following NIST CSF, companies can proactively identify and mitigate threats, improve incident response and recovery, and build customer trust by demonstrating a strong commitment to security. It is great to see that we are better positioned to supports scalability, secure software development, and fosters a culture of cybersecurity awareness across the organization. It also ensures resilience, minimizing downtime and operational disruptions in the event of an attack, while helping meet legal and regulatory requirements.

All of this becomes even more important for our customers given that our aim is for them to adopt our software solutions in the cloud and also provide solutions supported by AI.

entry.title: Re: Can you tell me anyone what is the solution of this e...
entry.content:
Hi Shubham, the error is reflecting that tax account key RA2 is not defined.

Request if you can check the Tax Code that you have leveraged in the posting does have any Account key RA2 in it and is GL Account defined for it.
Go to FTXP and verify the tax code - does it has any account key RA2 on it?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: How to generate seperate spool for each customer in F.27 - Periodic account statements
entry.content: This old thread was related to F.27 not FBL5N, better ask a new question.

entry.title: Re: Proof Of Delivery
entry.content:
Hello @Vivek_Savant 

I don't think you need to do anything else - the system data correctly reflects the business situation:

  1. 10 units have been issued and posted to cost of good sold; that is what has happened in the warehouse
  2. the customer has confirmed receipt of 8 units, i.e. that 2 units have vanished into thin air - maybe they have been lost or stolen during transit
  3. therefore you can only invoice 8 units

Unless, the missing 2 units are found, you don't need to do anything else.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: I need to add an additional us gaap ledger during a move ECC to a S/4HANA private cloud
entry.content:
Dear community members,

A customer of mine is about to move from its ECC system to a S/4HANA Private Cloud, and asked me if it is possible to add an US GaaP ledger in addition of its local ledger.

Anyone already faced this issue ?

Thanks for support 

BR

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024
entry.content: My buddy Sully

entry.title: License Consumption History in Customer Insights Dashboard
entry.content:
The contractual license consumption is a crucial part for gaining a comprehensive overview of your usage, adoption, and consumption patterns. Additionally to the current overview of the License Consumption a new section dedicated to License Consumption history has been introduced in Customer Insights reporting dashboard.

Accessing the License Consumption Section

You can access the section License Consumption in  ‘Customer Insights‘ dashboard  from the Entry page using the relevant 'Summary' card or by navigating to the “License Consumption” section in the Tab Menu. This section is divided into two tabs: one for current consumption and a new one for consumption history.

Understanding License Consumption History

The History of the License Consumption displays your contractual license consumption for the selected period for all measured license metrics. All metrics are categorized based on their consumption levels, allowing you to easily identify over-consumption or under-consumption: 

  • Potential Over-Consumption: Usage exceeds 100% of subscriptions.
  • Potential Under-Consumption: Low consumption (< 20%).
  • Regular License Consumption: Consumption between 20% and 100%.
  • Measured Consumption Without Subscriptions.

A monthly graph illustrates the total number of license metrics with specific consumption characteristics, enabling you to quickly identify trends across all subscriptions. This helps you determine whether regular consumption is growing or if low consumption is increasing, indicating a need for improvement.

 

Customization and Detailed Analysis

You can filter the data by time period or select a specific Solution Area to view consumption trends for particular product groups. Clicking on a segment or line in the chart displays all metrics belonging to the selected Consumption Intensity Type for the chosen month, providing detailed information in a table format.

The table includes additional details such as the number of subscriptions for each metric, links to metric descriptions and documentation (Customer Info Sheet), and consumption measurements. You can customize the table by adding more columns using the customization wheel icon. The report date indicates when the last updated consumption data was received.

Special Cases and Visualization Options

A special case comes up when a metric is being metered per Entitlement Set ID. In this case the same license metric would appear several times, once for each entitlement set and it can have different consumption intensity for different entitlement sets. If you use the filter to display such special metrics, please note that the line diagram might not be the best graph layout to use. The limitation here is that in case the same number of metrics has different consumption intensity, the different lines will overlap, and you only are able to see the line in front but not the line in the back.

For metrics metered per Entitlement Set ID, the same license metric may appear multiple times with different consumption intensities. In such cases, the line diagram might not be the best visualization due to overlapping lines. Switching to a stacked column chart can provide a clearer view of all values. You can easily change the graph layout to e.g. stacked column chart by clicking on the graph icon in the upper right corner.

Example:

Line Chart: Only 4 regular consumption metrics are visible for September in the graph.

Stacked Column Chart: Both 4 low and 4 regular consumption metrics are displayed.

Utilizing the License History Trend 

Use the new License History trend to overlook your overall consumption, compare different product groups and solutions and analyze single metrics history.

Use the License History trend to monitor overall consumption, compare different product groups and solutions, and analyse individual metrics history.

Stay updated by subscribing to this blog and visiting the SAP Support Portal / Customer Insights.

Your feedback is valuable to us. Please use the ‘Feedback’ slider on the right edge of the page of the application to share with us your feedback on this new tool and help us to improve the reporting functionality in SAP for Me.

Stay updated by subscribing to this blog and visiting the SAP Support Portal / Customer Insights.

SAP for Me is made for you!

Related blogs: SAP for Me goes reporting, License consumption in Customer Insights  or all related blogs to ‘Customer Insights’ reporting dashboard.

entry.title: Discussion forum closes soon!
entry.content:
Dear course participants,

Thank you for learning with us and posting your course related questions on this forum. 

We look forward to your posts, if you would like to address them before the course forum engagement ends on September 30, 2024. Our course experts are available until September 30, 2024 to answer your questions.  

Meanwhile, happy learning on our course " Modernizing Integration with SAP Integration Suite" and good luck with your quiz to earn your record of achievement!

Best regards,
Jayanthi
SAP Learning team

entry.title: Re: cross tab and non aggregated tab in story reports table
entry.content:
First of all the cross tab and non-aggregated tabs in story report table widgets are the ways through which we can arrange the columns in a specific format.
Then the implementation of the table widget depends upon the measures and dimensions we are trying to add in the implementation of the story report.

The Cross Tab functionality enables one to obtain a cross tabular representation of the data from a query used in the table report.
It specifically places columns in a two-dimensional row-column format. In this format we can align the columns such that the data related to the row and column can be represented as a single unit value of both the entities.

In contrast to this the non-aggregated column representation is the one which is helpful in performing a list-view of the table data. Meaning it will only help the person to view the data in simple table format with the columns as labels and the data in the form of rows.



Complex calculated measures that involve statistical calculations like aggregation, sum, minimum, maximum as well as quartile operations, all these can be created and represented in a structural format in Cross Tab. Whereas the non-aggregated format helps to place measures and dimensions in a single line of columns that can be rearranged from the table designer pane.

entry.title: Re: Legal Right to use RFC component
entry.content:
Hi @Sandra_Rossi , I'm referring to sap RFC functions itselves, not the connectors.

So, to be more precise, can i legally use an already installed third party RFC i.e. named: /XXX/GET_MY_CUSTOMERS ?

entry.title: Re: Building custom Excel view in SAPUI5 : Technical Part 1
entry.content:
Hi @TrusPatel ,
thank you for your blog post.

Unfortunately there are a few things in there that are not best practie:
Most importantly, your usage of third party libraries. It´s not just really bad practice to load a external js file from a CDN, it is also a very old version of this library. The current version is "0.20.3".
If you would have researched just a little bit you would have seen that.
They even have a section in their documentation: https://docs.sheetjs.com/docs/getting-started/installation/amd#sap-ui5
The best way is use ui5-tooling-modules: https://www.npmjs.com/package/ui5-tooling-modules
Your way of "integration" would not work in any Fiori Launchpad.

Why do you use that in "onInit"?

 

that = this;

 

 Why do you mix Hungarian notation and non Hungarian notation?
Why do you use "var"? 
Why do you use all the controls like sap.m.Table directly?

new sap.m.Table

 You switch between namespaces between sample files, why?

By large, this is a very bad example of code. Please never use this code in a productive.

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Build – Q3 2024 Edition
entry.content:
Greetings, Builders! We’re pleased to bring you the Q3 Edition of our What’s New in SAP Build series. In this edition, we’re covering product-specific updates for:

  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Build Apps
  • SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Start, SAP Task Center
  • SAP Build Code

SAP Build Process Automation

SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud: The extensibility wizard is now available with the 2408 edition of SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. This makes the creation of side-by-side extensions even more streamlined and allows you to seamlessly navigate to SAP Build from your Cloud ERP. This first release enables custom process creation, with more options from SAP Build to become available this year. See more in the blog.

Scheduled trigger: Now you can schedule process automations based on user-defined time intervals and dates at runtime. This allows you to enable system-centric and time-based process automation use cases, such as working and nonworking days in creating your automation scenarios to avoid resource mismatches.

Additional Outcomes in Forms and UI5 Support for Tasks: This enables citizen developers to configure additional outcome actions in the process to let approvers request rework in case of missing or wrong information in approval forms. You can also start filling out multiple forms efficiently through a guided experience.

Advanced Event Project Creation with Manual Specification Upload: This allows users to upload business event specifications for the creation of an event project in the lobby. The new feature adds support for business events across lines of business (LOBs) and custom events, including third-party events that are supported at runtime using SAP Event Mesh.

Process Visibility enhancements: These include actionable insights, instance tracking for requestors and additional monitoring capabilities via CALM. Now you can turn insights into meaningful outcomes and enable the triggering of escalation flows based on visibility insights, to directly react and resolve critical situations. This enables auditable and governed escalation resolution, instead of having disconnected communication through different channels.

More on SAP Build Process Automation:

  • See the What's New Viewer for more updates, and improvements.
  • See SAP Road Map Explorer for more upcoming features and focus areas.

SAP Build Apps

Release management and project lifecycle management: It’s now possible to release and transport projects across different tenants using SAP Cloud Transport Management System. You can transport project versions between subaccounts while isolating development, testing, and productive deployment of SAP Build Apps applications to their own landscapes. To get started, see this blog post.

Performant table list with large dataset from S/4HANA: Managing and displaying large amounts of data in an application can be challenging and cause performance issues. Check out this example of creating a performant list page for an S/4HANA Cloud endpoint with 65k+ records - see the blog post.

Copying pages within one project: This feature enables users to copy and duplicate a page within a project in SAP Build Apps – a simple, yet powerful, add-on to further speed up development.

New Build Service: We recently launched a new and improved experience for deploying application packages. The build service includes managing image assets, iOS usage strings, mobile service enablement, package creation, and configuration management. The updated UI provides a much smoother experience for developers at the deployment phase, ensuring that every requirement is met and that your apps are ready to publish. Try it out today!

  • Also check out Ludo Noens’ series on Consuming SAP with SAP Build Apps – Creating a CRUD Application
  • Learn more about what’s coming up on the SAP Build Apps Roadmap.

SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Start, and SAP Task Center

SAP Build Work Zone, both editions

HTML5 Business Solution as a Content Provider: With the latest update in SAP Build Work Zone, you can now expose BTP-based applications as Work Zone content, like roles, spaces, and pages, in the 'Common Data Model' (CDM) format. As a result, you can easily configure and enable access to BTP applications from a Work Zone instance, even across subaccounts. See more in the Help Portal and this blog post.

SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition

Define Sort Order of pages and spaces: Administrators can now configure how spaces and pages are sorted on the menu bar of SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition. This feature is only applicable if you're working in the new experience view mode for spaces and pages. See more in the Help Portal.

Import and Export of Pages and Spaces: Content manager tools will be enhanced to enable administrators to export and import custom (locally created) spaces and pages between landscapes.

SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition

Integrated guided experience across SAP Build Work Zone and Process Automation*: Guided processes are now available as an artifact in SAP Build Process Automation. In addition to the previously available “no template” wizards, it is now possible to create guided, process-based wizards in SAP Build Work Zone.

*Note: This will become GA in October 2024. 

SAP Start

Directly navigate to product home pages of integrated cloud solutions: The SAP Start site offers a new product section to easily see and navigate to the product home pages of the integrated solutions. This enables transparency of available cloud solutions for the current user, and quick and easy navigation to launch the product home page of the desired solution.

SAP Task Center

ITCE partner page: SAP Task Center now allows connections with third-party task providers. The first solution to leverage this capability is TaskConnect, developed by ITCE in collaboration with SAP architects and technical consultants. The application will soon be available on the SAP Store as well, offering a comprehensive configuration guide, installation manuals and BPMN diagrams. For more information about TaskConnect, see here.

More on SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Start and SAP Task Center:

  • Check out the roadmaps for SAP Build Work Zone standard & advanced edition and SAP Start / SAP Task Center
  • What's New Viewers: Standard edition / Advanced edition / SAP Start / SAP Task Center 

SAP Build Code

New AI Features: In SAP Build Code, you can now use the Joule copilot, our generative AI assistant, to write code by providing natural language prompts. For a detailed explanation of Joule, please read this article. In the last months we added many new features to this capability including the following highlights:

  • Support SAP S/4HANA Cloud Service Integration for Data Model and App Logic: It is now possible to use Joule to generate code for your integrated SAP S/4HANA Cloud services.
  • Major UX Update of Joule in SAP Build Code: With the latest UX update of Joule within SAP Build Code we enhanced the usability, streamlined workflows, and created a more intuitive interface, boosting productivity and satisfaction.
  • Generative AI support for productivity tools: We now allow you to use Joule directly from the storyboard.
  • Use Joule to enhance your already existing data models and services: It is now possible to modify already existing data model and service entities using a prompt.

Why SAP Build Code: Check out this blog post by Product Manager Rupa Maity describing why to move to SAP Build Code.

Stay updated on the latest Build Code innovations: Do you want to stay informed about the latest product updates for SAP Build Code? Follow our tag on SAP Community and subscribe to our SAP Build Code Newsletter. 

  • See the latest update product updates from September 2024 for SAP Build Code.
  • Learn more on the SAP Build Code Community.

--- 

💡Please note this blog post provides a highlight of the latest features and enhancements. 

  • For comprehensive list of all updates, please refer to our Help Portal What’s New in SAP Build.
  • Join the SAP Builders group for product resources and community questions.
  • Devtoberfest is here! 🥨 Don't miss our SAP Build-themed sessions and more.
  • ...and that means SAP TechEd is right around the corner! Explore SAP Build at TechEd 2024.

Which feature are you most excited to test out? Let us know in the comments!

entry.title: Re: Custom Fiori application tile not found in Content Ma...

entry.title: Changes in the Communication of the Electronic Nota Fiscal for Electrical Energy
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

The SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing option for Brazil team has released updates on the communication of the Electronic Nota Fiscal for Electrical Energy (NF3-e) solution with SEFAZ.

NF3-e Technical Note 2024.002 1.01 Version

This update complies with the SEFAZ NF3-e Technical Note 2024.002 1.01 Version, which announced the discontinuation of the asynchronous authorization service, also known as the batch submission service. The communication between the SAP service and SEFAZ now is synchronous.

This communication change has been updated in the outbound invoicing option for Brazil service, and it is not necessary to implement SAP Notes in your backend system. For official communication on this update, read the SAP Note 3509349.

Check out the latest updates and features in our What's New session. And for a step-by-step guide on how to use the Electronic Nota Fiscal for Electrical Energy (NF3-e) solution, refer to the following SAP Help Portal pages:

  • Integration > NF3-e Specifics
  • Using Outbound Nota Fiscal > NF3-e Specifics

Related blog posts:

  • Usage Analytics Dashboard
  • How to Easily Browse the SAP Help Portal Pages for Outbound Nota Fiscal
  • An Introduction to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Navigating the SAP Help Portal

You can sign up for email alerts when new SAP Notes are released or updated in the components listed below; just follow the instructions in SAP Knowledge Base Article 2478289.

  • CA-GTF-CSC-EDO-BR-OU (Electronic Nota Fiscal for Electrical Energy, NF3-e)
  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-OUT (Nota Fiscal Eletrônica Outbound)

Use the regulatory change manager tool to find out if a solution to a legal change is planned. Take a look at SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 and the regulatory change manager page to understand how to use this tool.

Have you found this blog post useful? If so, choose “like” (kudos), share it with your colleagues, and follow me for similar content. You are welcome to leave your questions and read similar blog posts on the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance SAP Community. Don’t forget to follow the SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica) tag and the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance community to keep up with the latest NF-e news.

Thank you, and until next time,
Thiago

Tags: brazil, brasil, einvoice, einvoicing, e-invoicing, electronic invoice, electronic invoicing, edocument, edoc, e-document, e-docs, e-doc, e-docs, drc, document compliance, nota fiscal eletrônica, electronic nota fiscal, outbound, nf3-e, nf-e, electric energy, energia elétrica, is-u, nota técnica 2024.002, 2024/002, 2024.002

entry.title: Re: Emarsys AI content generator
entry.content:
This feature and a few others from the What's New page below are available via Pilot program. The features are not yet widely available for all customers as they are still in testing phase but interested customers can apply to the program.

You can check with your Emarsys CSM and they will be able to help you with the application.

entry.title: SAP Service Cloud Workflow field update use string in the formula
entry.content:

Is it possible to have a blank space between it? 
I tried with " or ' but it is not working, it is showing a syntax error... 

Does somebody have a tip?

Thanks a lot!

Deborah

entry.title: Re: 🟢 Understand the Clean Core Extensibility Options for Cloud ERP

In addition, please refer the individual to the deep dive sessioon on key user extensibility in week 3: What Software Developers Should Know About Key User Extensibility . 

Hope this helps.

entry.title: SAP Product Inspiration Series for Industries & Intelligent CX (Wave #4)
entry.content:
Hi everyone! Today I'm more than excited to let you know we go full steam ahead with our Product Inspiration Video Series into the 2nd half of the year and covering the latest innovations of SAP Intelligent CX along with S/4HANA, Business AI, Joule, BTP and industry-tailored solutions. Please find the latest deep dive videos from our product teams below.

 

1. Mediafly AI Coaching & Enablement for SAP Sales Cloud & Service Cloud Version 2

Ben Turner, Director Product Management at Mediafly provides an overview on AI Coaching and Enablement for SAP Customer Experience. Watch this product inspiration video to learn about the strategic direction of Mediafly in building this solution with latest integrations to SAP Sales and Service Cloud Version 2 as well as our overall innovations as part of the Intelligent CX portfolio.

The key highlights shared in this video are:
00:00 - Introduction
00:26 - Why Mediafly and SAP Sales and Service Cloud?

03:46 - What is your overall direction in terms of AI in Mediafly and SAP?

11:19 - Demo: Mediafly Content Shares

Watch this product inspiration video to learn about customer service processes for utilities, how these are deeply connected to SAP S/4HANA as well as our major innovations as part of the Intelligent CX portfolio from SAP.

The key highlights shared in this video are:

17:00 - Demo: Start new Move-In Process

18:08 - Demo: Move-In Process - Select Services
18:21 - Demo: Move-In Process - Contract Account and Billing

Watch this product inspiration video to learn about the strategic direction and importance of SAP Enterprise Service Management for Finance scenarios as well as our major innovations as part of the Intelligent CX portfolio.

The key highlights shared in this video are:

4. SAP Enterprise Service Management for HR

Akshay Shripathi, Senior Product Manager SAP Enterprise Service Management at SAP provides an overview of SAP ESM, the new shared service framework for Finance and HR. entry.title: Re: Host a CodeJam in Berlin
entry.content: Thank you for the quick answer. We are fine with a CodeJam in January next year. Looking forward to the preparation and then the session.

entry.title: Job interval impact for transport creation, release and import from SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hi, 

according to this page:https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/setup-administration/setting-up-managed-systems?locale=en-US

The data transfer between on-premise SAP system to SAP Cloud ALM, is always the direction that on-premise system PUSH data to Cloud ALM.

Does it mean that, if I follow the default collection interval as defined in /n/SDF/ALM_SETUP, then I need to wait the corresponding minutes for the transports to be created / released / imported in the backend on premise system, after I perform the action in Cloud ALM?

For example, when I create a transport in SAP Cloud ALM, then I need to wait maximum 10 minutes, before the transport can be created in backend on-premise system?

if yes, what's the risk, if I shorten the interval to 1 minute?

I mean, currently in SAP Solution Manager ChaRM (Change and Request Management), it's a syncronous process. When I trigger transport creation in ChaRM, it's created at the same time in the backend SAP system, and I know from ChaRM immediately if the creation is successfully or not.
When it comes to SAP Cloud ALM, if this is an asyncronous process, it's very user unfriendly. Firstly I need to wait up to 10 minutes for the transport to be created, and then another 10 minutes for the status to be reflected in Cloud ALM.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Job interval impact for transport creation, release a...
entry.content:
Yes, the interval of the job for Implementation features has a direct impact on the time it takes to create, release, and import Transport Requests. The default values are set with high numbers, but can be changed to lower numbers if desired. 

The SAP Help Portal documentation for SAP Cloud ALM outlines the following in section Configuring the PUSH Data Provider (just above section Configuration of the Transport Organizer Web UI😞
Note
For your test or productive landscape, set the collection interval to 1 min for these use cases if you want a quicker reaction to your testing of creating transport request, transport of copies, and triggering the deploy in the features. 

SAP Cloud ALM is not an on-premise system, and as such it does not use the same technology to communicate with the on-premise systems. It must use asynchronous communication to trigger actions and receive updates from on-premise systems. 

Reducing the job interval time carries the same risks as scheduling any periodic job at very short intervals. If the jobs have to trigger a lot of tasks or transmit a lot of data back to SAP Cloud ALM, this can consume on-premise system resources and impact performance. If this happens, the configuration can be altered to increase the interval value at small increments (1 minute at a time) to find a balance between on-premise system performance and SAP Cloud ALM update wait times. If the on-premise performance impact is severe and seems like an issue, a Case should be created to investigate in case an issue or bug is involved.

entry.title: Feature Request: Enrichment of attribute names in Signavio Process Intelligence
entry.content:
Hi,

I would like to address the following feature request:

Problem: When working with business objects in Signavio Process Intelligence our users sometimes struggle with relating the attribute names to the actual data in SAP. Some users prefer the naming in "natural language", others prefer the technical names (table and field names). 

Current situation:

1) We are currently maintaining a documentation aside from the actual Intelligence Dashboards where we translate the natural language names to the field names and vice versa. This documentation is not accepted well, as users want to have the information in the tool where they actually work in.

2) For one dashboard we have named the attributes with both: The natural language plus the table and field name. This resolves the issue of having to look things up in a different space, but results in very long column/attribute names and lots of waisted space in the dashboard: 

Feature Request:

We would like to request some sort of rule, that - if applied in the backend - results in splitting the attribute names by a given parameter (eg. a | (pipe symbol)), so that the part before the pipe becomes the actual attribute name, and the part after the name can be retrieved through a mouse-over, wherever the attribute is used (e.g. in the filtering options, in column names when displaying data in tables, etc...):

This would save space in the actual dashboard and still allow for additional context where needed.

Best, Anna

entry.title: Using Contract functionality to give first 5 orders as free
entry.content:
Hi, 

 

We have a special condition type ( price). A material is created to charge this fees to customer. We create a debit note and enter this material to charge customer. The requirement is to make sure that first 5 orders which are created should be free and prices should only be picked from 6th order. Prices are getting picked from VK11 only. Can we use contract functionality for this?

entry.title: How to get "marked for Deletion" and "Blocked Items of a Purchase Order via the API?
entry.content:
Hello!

I need to get the Purchase Order Items via the Purchase Order OData V4 SAP Public Cloud API. However, marked for deletion and blocked items aren't retrieved by the API. Or at least not in the same way as the active items.

I have found this note that describe the same issue. However, that note only refers to SAP S/4 HANA Cloud 2208 and we are in 2408. Just wondering if the Note also applies to newer versions or if there is any secret API call to also get the PO Items marked for Deletion/Blocked.

Thanks a lot in advance!

entry.title: Re: What data can you use Bulk Uploads for?
entry.content: Thank you for the suggestion. We do already use these templates, however we're thinking of combining templates. E.g Job / Position changes including a change of Pay, how would this work and affect the position management. Would one template be able to be used or would many templates need to be uploaded.

entry.title: Re: Internal behaviour of dump marker and rsync marker in...
entry.content:
I'm confused. To setup a database subscription replicate (MSA) for a primary where user updates are active, this is how I know to do things (using bulk (dump/load) materialization):

In the primary db, make sure to run sp_reptostandby <db>,'all'

If the primary db is not part of a warm standby replication setup, make sure to run sp_config_rep_agent <db>,"send warm standby xacts"

Make sure syslogins match on the source and destination ASE servers

Run rs_init to setup a connection to the dest db

Create a database replication definition (if not already done)

Setup empty schema at the dest db (is this necessary?)

Define the subscription using a dump_marker:

define subscription <DEST_ASE>_<db>
for database replication definition <db>
with primary at <logical_conn>.<db>
with replicate at <DEST_ASE>.<db>
subscribe to truncate table /* optional. Default is false */
use dump marker

rs_helpdbsub will show "Defined" for the PRS and RRS at this point

Do a dump/load copy from the primary to the new MSA replicate.  At the end of the dump, a dump marker is written to the primary db.  When the dump marker makes it to the DSI connection, the connection will automatically suspend.  rs_helpdbsub will show "Valid" for the DSI connection

Load the dump to the destination db.  If there are triggers in the db, disable them using: alter connection to <DEST_ASE>.<db> set dsi_keep_triggers to 'off';

 

Run check subscription

In the MSA replicate db make sure the secondary truncation point is disabled so the transaction log won't fill up: dbcc settrunc('ltm', 'ignore') )

Resume the DSI connection to the new MSA replicate db.

Using this scenario, there's no need for a resync marker, right?

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Is it possible to switch my CDS view (odata v2) for POs to OData v4?
entry.content: Using RAP, you can have multiple OData services for one entity, both v2 and v4.

entry.title: Re: S_ALR_87013047 with matnr more than 18 digits.
entry.content:
So look at 831966 - Restrictions when Implementing the Long Material Number LMN 

  • 2. Restrictions in application areas Finance (FI) and Controlling (CO) of SAP ERP:
  • - Generated reports cannot deal with LMN.
  • In such a case, check for existing solutions provided with SAP notes for application area IS-A-LMN or send a customer message in order to request support.

entry.title: Change length of column in table in SAP BTP ABAP environment gives ATC error Length of filed changed
entry.content:
I changed custom table  field(char) length  from 5 to 6 in BTP . It is already having entries .Activated successfully. While trying to release the task from Dev, gives ATC error "Length of field component is changed".

After I did a Quick Fix by Adjust database and convert data also same error occurs.

Kindly provide a solution for this

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano 

I've posted this previously but thought I'd do it again to ensure I'm enrolled in the SAP Digital Skills Initiative.

I've been in IT for many decades and was one of the first people offered a job for SAP USA back in the late 1980's.  However, I declined because it was 'a startup!'  Please don't laugh at me.  

Anyway, I've worked in IT from Developer to Architect and in Management and Infrastructure capacity on IBM systems, as well as ERP and CRM systems from competitors or industry specific offerings.  

I seem to be too old for Corporate but way too young to retire.  That said, I do meet some of the requirements of those underrepresented.  

    • Women
    • First generation college students (although I've obtained my degree long ago)
    • People looking to switch their careers mid-life
    • People who lack the financial means to afford a certification attempt for themselves

I most definitely meet the prerequisite as I always have a willingness to learn more about many technologies, including SAP, and I'm willing to undergo the rigors of studying for SAP Certification.

Thanks so much for your time and consideration, and for offering this program!  Hope I can accomplish something in the time allotted since I've only found this site recently.

entry.title: Re: Has anyone deployed Vertex with HA?
entry.content: Dear rhamodiong1129, This is a very original topic and I don’t think that there is a straight answer to this. This topic requires SAP and Vertex Architecture level discussion. As standard, Vertex is connected with SAP via SIC e.g. SAP Integration Component. An RFC call is made from SAP to SIC and SIC has a configuration for the destination of Vertex Oseries. You are expecting that in case of an emergency, the Vertex connection in SIC should move from Vertex Oseries hosted in AZ1 to AZ2 or vice versa. I believe you need to contact Vertex and engage with their architecture team. I don’t think this is possible as of now but you can check with them. Thanks Avnish

Dear community members,

Many thanks support

BR

entry.title: SAP Business One - equalization tax
entry.content: I can't find how to add the sales equalization tax for suppliers. We buy porducts and sell them in the same status, so we have to pay that tax to the suppliers when we get stock. The question is, where can I do it? When I add the invoice in the system, that information should be showed as the taxes do. So how can I show it? How do I have to configurate our SAP Business 1 to show it? I have located it in the clients screen but not for suppliers.

entry.title: Re: Usage of "JustAsk" within the story in a separate panel.
entry.content:
Hi Ramkumar,
Great question !

Yes,  you are correct, there are currently no API for just ask. This is something we are considering for a future release but this is not planned for the upcoming Q4, Q1 25 releases.

In parallel, we are planning to integrate Joule in SAP Analytics Cloud. This will be an story assistant available as a side panel in the story both in view and edit mode. Check the video from the Generative AI for Planning & Analytics page.
Would the address your use case  ?
It would be great if you could provide some details  on your scenario for story script API to invoke just ask.

Thanks,
François

Thanks for any suggestions.

entry.title: SSAM Catalog Profile Selection
entry.content:
Hello Experts 

I need some quick inputs regarding SAP SSAM

We are using latest version 2405 of SSAM with Phase model enabled, while creating Reactive maintenance notification catalog profile selection is not working as it should, catalog profile selection is influenced by notification type assignment in SPRO, and not at technical object level, this works fine in back end S/4 system, I had checked settings in SSAM config panel for order of selection for catalog profile which  is configured as "Eq,FL,Notification", 

Please suggest where else should i check for the settings

Thanks in Advance 

BR

Vivek K

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024
entry.content: Looks like he’s in sales

entry.title: Re: Make attachment mandatory in me51n
entry.content:
Are you describing the GOS button?

  • Look for a pre-save/check BAdI method (e.g. ME_PROCESS_REQ_CUST~CHECK)
  • There call the GOS to check existence of attachment, either get a reference to current gos or use a generic class
  • If none raise an error

Some hints

" Get GOS reference
" - method im_header->get_gos_manager
" or simple class
" - method cl_binary_relation=>read_links_of_objects

" Raise an error
INCLUDE mm_messages_mac.
MESSAGE e123(zmyclass) INTO gl_dummy.
mmpur_message_forced 'E' 'ZMYCLASS' '123' '' '' '' ''. " 4 values allowed
im_header->invalidate( ).

entry.title: Re: Show count of selected items in ListPicker
entry.content: Are you wanting to automatically select all items in the District for example when selecting a State or just filtering the Districts based on the state so when the user goes to make a selection they can pick from only those associated to the State?

entry.title: Duplicate spool is getting generated in MIGO for Mtyp 311.
entry.content:
Hello Friends

When i am trying to do transfer posting in MIGO, movement type 311 for custom Output , duplicate spools are getting generated which is causing paper wastage.

Replication steps.Step 1. Run the tcode MIGO
Step 2. Choose A08 Transfer posting, R10 Other
Step 3. Enter material <XXXXXXXXXX>, Plant XXXX, Issuing Storage location XXXX, Destination storage location XXXX and Quantity 1.
Step 4. Click on Post.

Did debugging there is no code to generate duplicate spools.

And even choosing Individual slip while posting.

 

Regards

Naeeum

entry.title: Direct Data Access to SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
CDP team is in the process of procuring "SAP Customer Data Platform, advanced insights (DIGITS) add-on" to connect CDP instance with Datasphere instance using Direct Data Access feature.

Documentation: Direct Data Access

Current CDP business units are in EU5 (Netherlands - cf-eu20) and Datasphere is in Europe (Frankfurt – AWS).

Please confirm, will the standard Direct Data Access feature allow connection between these two instances. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Direct Data Access to SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
According to documentation in SAP Datasphere:

SAP Datasphere Configuration

SAP Customer Data Platform is available in two data centers:

  • Europe (Netherlands) - cf-eu20
  • US East (VA) - cf-us21

The corresponding CDP HANA Cloud instances are also located on those two BTP data centers. Therefore, SAP Customer Data Platform Direct Data Access connectivity is currently supported for SAP Datasphere instances located on one of the mentioned BTP data centers.

According to documentation, set up on your side should work.

To activate Direct Data Access please contact your Account manager to assist you with enabling this feature.

Additional documentation:

2626344 - Who is the SAP Account Manager/Executive for my company?

3359101 - How to activate Purpose View in CDP 

Direct Data Access

entry.title: Re: Using class CL_ABAP_PARALLEL for mass parallel dialog work processes
entry.content: Thank you for this useful blog.

entry.title: Re: Central Finance Tips and Tricks #6 - Managing AIF Errors
entry.content:
Hi Marc,

one question to usage of BADI /AIF/ALERT_DET_RECIPIENTS with BRF+. How did you get the Key Field 1 RAW values into the BADI? I achieved to get, via the delivered log, the message content but there the value is already "translated" from LIFNR to "ERP Vendor Number" which makes a rule definition a bit hard.

BR

Jan

entry.title: TabStrip in Popup does not work in different pages of the SAC Story
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I have Story Popup created with TabStrip and several tabs. I want to use them among different pages created in the SAC Story but it says that TabStrip can not be used as is on different page.

Popup_4.open();
TabStrip_2.setSelectedKey("Tab_4");

See attached the error message.

I have to copy the PopUp in all Canvas pages in order to work, is that a way to avoid so many tables to be duplicated?

entry.title: Re: Show count of selected items in ListPicker
entry.content:
Hi Bill,

Automatic selection should only happen in Village listpicker. For all other listpickers user will manually select the options.

In addition to auto selection of values for Village listpicker, it should show value as "Village 1, Village 2 + 100 more" not Village1, Village2, Village3, Village4 as the current behaviour of listpicker is.

entry.title: Re: How to keep open date live when running spool in FBL5N?
entry.content: Define a variant with dynamic calculation for this field, or just save it without data for the field if SAP provide correct date by default.

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 1 Contest Activities
entry.content: @vladimirs_semikins I've reported that one to IT to research.

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 1 Contest Activities
entry.content: @ymAllen I have reported that badge for you to IT and they will research.

entry.title: Re: Start Your scripting Journey The Easy Way With SAP Analytics Cloud | Part Two
entry.content: @nguyen_thanh_hieu just add a Text Object then select the icon, Right Mouse Click and "Add Dynamic Text" - you cand add a User Name, Date, Time and a whole host of other dynamic options.

entry.title: Volume /hana/log is full with log segments in state FREE (2765 files / 1GB)
entry.content:
Hello everyone,
after a hang log_backup, our logsegments on the HDB under /hana/log/SID/mnt00001/hdb00003.00003 were no longer backed up for about 14 days.

Now the backup is running correctly again, but there are now 2265 logsegments (files) with the status FREE and we had to enlarge the affected volume several times.

The individual log segments are now being overwritten/reused step by step, but there are no fewer of them, so the volume is still nearly full.

hdblogdiag seglist /hana/log/SID/mnt00001/hdb00003.00003 | grep -i '\bFree\b' | wc -l
2765 (number of files in state free)

According to sapnote 2083715, we can do this with "ALTER SYSTEM RECLAIM LOG;"
"You can free the space on your file system occupied by the allocated log segments by executing the following SQL statement: ALTER SYSTEM RECLAIM LOG;"

https://me.sap.com/notes/2083715/D

What general conditions apply to the command?
Does the SAP Application have to be offline for this?

I'm looking forward to your feedback.
Kind regards, Martin
